name: Tarte Au Citron
recipeInstructions:
1. Roll out half the dough to 1/8-inch thickness on a floured surface.
2. Press it firmly into an 8-inch tart pan with a removable rim.
3. Trim excess dough, leaving a 1/2-inch overhang.
4. Fold the overhang back inside the rim and press to the side of the shell.
5. Push the dough up 1/4 inch above the edge of the pan. Crimp decoratively with a fork.
6. Roll out remaining dough and make another tart shell using the same method.
7. Chill shells for 60 minutes.
8. Line shells with foil, fill with rice, and bake in the lower third of a preheated 400°F oven for 8 minutes.
9. Remove rice and foil, then bake for 8-10 more minutes or until pale golden.
10. Remove shells from pans, transfer to a rack, and let cool.
11. In a bowl with an electric mixer, beat egg yolks with 1/2 cup sugar until thick and pale, forming a ribbon.
12. Beat in zest and lemon juice.
13. Transfer mixture to a heavy saucepan. Cook over medium-low heat until it registers 140°F on a candy thermometer.
14. Continue cooking at 140°F for 3 minutes longer; do not boil.
15. Remove from heat, let cool while covered with buttered wax paper.
16. In another bowl, beat egg whites with salt until holding soft peaks.
17. Add remaining sugar one tablespoon at a time, beating until stiff peaks form.
18. Stir a quarter of the meringue into the lemon mixture and fold in the remaining gently but thoroughly.
19. Transfer tart shells to a baking sheet and fill with lemon mixture, mounding slightly.
20. Bake tarts in the middle of a preheated 400°F oven for 12-15 minutes or until tops are golden and puffed.
21. Transfer tarts to a rack and let cool.
